text:
<@U0BDGF43EBX> Sprint 1 goal: Build the multi-tenant foundation for PulseDesk.
project root - E:\forge2-pulsedesk-starter-kit\forge2-Shankar

Tasks needed:
1. Organization model + migration (id, name, slug, timestamps)
2. User model with organization_id FK, role enum (admin/agent/customer), Sanctum tokens
3. Register API: POST /api/auth/register (creates org + admin user together)
4. Login API: POST /api/auth/login (returns Bearer token)
5. Logout API: POST /api/auth/logout
6. EnsureSameTenant middleware — derives org from auth()->user()->organization_id NEVER from request
7. Pest tests: register, login, cross-tenant isolation (org A user cannot see org B data)

text:
*ask #1 — assigned to OpenClaw*

*What to build:* Organization & User Models with Migrations

*Details:* Create Organization and User models, migrations, and relationships. Organization is the tenant root; User belongs to Organization. Add unique constraints on organization slug and user email per tenant. Include hasMany / belongsTo relations and fillable casts.

*Files to touch:* app/Models/Organization.php, app/Models/User.php, database/migrations/2024_01_01_000001_create_organizations_table.php, database/migrations/2024_01_01_000002_create_users_table.php, database/factories/OrganizationFactory.php, database/factories/UserFactory.php

When done, post to <#C0BDKRX9EM8|agent-log> and include the word DONE.
[1:39 PM] :clipboard: *Task #2 — assigned to OpenClaw*

*What to build:* Auth API Endpoints (Register, Login, Logout)

*Details:* Implement Sanctum-based auth controllers for register, login, logout. Registration creates Organization + User together. Issue plain-text token via Sanctum. Add routes under api/v1 and request validation classes.

*Files to touch:* app/Http/Controllers/Api/V1/Auth/RegisterController.php, app/Http/Controllers/Api/V1/Auth/LoginController.php, app/Http/Controllers/Api/V1/Auth/LogoutController.php, app/Http/Requests/Auth/RegisterRequest.php, app/Http/Requests/Auth/LoginRequest.php, routes/api.php, config/sanctum.php

When done, post to <#C0BDKRX9EM8|agent-log> and include the word DONE.
[1:40 PM] :clipboard: *Task #3 — assigned to OpenClaw*

*What to build:* EnsureSameTenant Middleware

*Details:* Create middleware that resolves the tenant from the authenticated user and guards against cross-tenant data access on tenant-scoped resources. Register it in Kernel and apply to tenant-scoped route group.

*Files to touch:* app/Http/Middleware/EnsureSameTenant.php, app/Http/Kernel.php, app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php

When done, post to <#C0BDKRX9EM8|agent-log> and include the word DONE.
[1:44 PM] :clipboard: *Task #4 — assigned to OpenClaw*

*What to build:* API Controllers

*Details:* Create RESTful controllers with CRUD methods for core resources

*Files to touch:* app/Http/Controllers/OrganizationController.php, app/Http/Controllers/UserController.php, app/Http/Controllers/ProjectController.php

When done, post to <#C0BDKRX9EM8|agent-log> and include the word DONE.

*Hermes*  [2:01 PM]
:clipboard: *Task #4 — assigned to OpenClaw*

*What to build:* API Endpoint Implementation

*Details:* Continue from Task 3 - implement RESTful API endpoints for CRUD operations

*Files to touch:* app/Http/Controllers/ApiController.php, routes/api.php

When done, post to <#C0BDKRX9EM8|agent-log> and include the word DONE.

text:
<@U0BDGF43EBX> Plan and start Sprint 2: Core Tickets CRUD, Conversation Threads, and Seeded Demo Data.

Break this sprint down into issues for:
1. Ticket Model & Migration: Create Ticket model with organization_id, subject, description, status (open, pending, resolved, closed), priority (low, medium, high, urgent), requester_id (user), assignee_id (user), and tags. Ensure organization_id is automatically scoped.
2. Comment Model & Migration: Create Comment model for threaded replies on tickets. Must include ticket_id, user_id, body, and is_internal (boolean for agent-only notes).
3. Tickets API Controller: Implement CRUD routes. Index must be filterable by status, priority, and assignee_id, with basic text search on subject/description. Ensure cross-tenant data leak checks are strictly enforced.
4. Comments API Controller: Implement endpoints to view ticket replies and post a reply (public) or internal note (is_internal = true, restricted to admins/agents).
5. Database Seeder: Create a DatabaseSeeder that populates 1 organization, 1 Admin, 2 Agents, 2 Customers, and exactly 12 tickets with a mix of statuses, priorities, assignees, and threaded replies (some public, some internal).
6. Integration Tests: Write Pest/PHPUnit feature tests verifying ticket CRUD, comment postings, and testing that Customer roles cannot see internal notes.

elements.text:
Plan and start Sprint 2: Core Tickets CRUD, Conversation Threads, and Seeded Demo Data.

Break this sprint down into issues for:
1. Ticket Model & Migration: Create Ticket model with organization_id, subject, description, status (open, pending, resolved, closed), priority (low, medium, high, urgent), requester_id (user), assignee_id (user), and tags. Ensure organization_id is automatically scoped.
2. Comment Model & Migration: Create Comment model for threaded replies on tickets. Must include ticket_id, user_id, body, and is_internal (boolean for agent-only notes).
3. Tickets API Controller: Implement CRUD routes. Index must be filterable by status, priority, and assignee_id, with basic text search on subject/description. Ensure cross-tenant data leak checks are strictly enforced.
4. Comments API Controller: Implement endpoints to view ticket replies and post a reply (public) or internal note (is_internal = true, restricted to admins/agents).
5. Database Seeder: Create a DatabaseSeeder that populates 1 organization, 1 Admin, 2 Agents, 2 Customers, and exactly 12 tickets with a mix of statuses, priorities, assignees, and threaded replies (some public, some internal).
6. Integration Tests: Write Pest/PHPUnit feature tests verifying ticket CRUD, comment postings, and testing that Customer roles cannot see internal notes.

text:
<@U0BDGF43EBX> Plan and start Sprint 2: Core Tickets CRUD, Conversation Threads, and Seeded Demo Data.

Break this sprint down into issues for:
1. Ticket Model & Migration: Create Ticket model with organization_id, subject, description, status (open, pending, resolved, closed), priority (low, medium, high, urgent), requester_id (user), assignee_id (user), and tags. Ensure organization_id is automatically scoped.
2. Comment Model & Migration: Create Comment model for threaded replies on tickets. Must include ticket_id, user_id, body, and is_internal (boolean for agent-only notes).
3. Tickets API Controller: Implement CRUD routes. Index must be filterable by status, priority, and assignee_id, with basic text search on subject/description. Ensure cross-tenant data leak checks are strictly enforced.
4. Comments API Controller: Implement endpoints to view ticket replies and post a reply (public) or internal note (is_internal = true, restricted to admins/agents).
5. Database Seeder: Create a DatabaseSeeder that populates 1 organization, 1 Admin, 2 Agents, 2 Customers, and exactly 12 tickets with a mix of statuses, priorities, assignees, and threaded replies (some public, some internal).
6. Integration Tests: Write Pest/PHPUnit feature tests verifying ticket CRUD, comment postings, and testing that Customer roles cannot see internal notes.

1. Ticket Model & Migration: Create Ticket model with organization_id, subject, description, status (open, pending, resolved, closed), priority (low, medium, high, urgent), requester_id (user), assignee_id (user), and tags. Ensure organization_id is automatically scoped.
2. Comment Model & Migration: Create Comment model for threaded replies on tickets. Must include ticket_id, user_id, body, and is_internal (boolean for agent-only notes).
3. Tickets API Controller: Implement CRUD routes. Index must be filterable by status, priority, and assignee_id, with basic text search on subject/description. Ensure cross-tenant data leak checks are strictly enforced.
4. Comments API Controller: Implement endpoints to view ticket replies and post a reply (public) or internal note (is_internal = true, restricted to admins/agents).
5. Database Seeder: Create a DatabaseSeeder that populates 1 organization, 1 Admin, 2 Agents, 2 Customers, and exactly 12 tickets with a mix of statuses, priorities, assignees, and threaded replies (some public, some internal).
6. Integration Tests: Write Pest/PHPUnit feature tests verifying ticket CRUD, comment postings, and testing that Customer roles cannot see internal notes.

text:
<@U0BDGF43EBX> Plan and start Sprint 3: SLA Policies, Queues & Assignment, Activity Log, and Dashboard Metrics.

Break this sprint down into issues for:
1. SLA Policies & Timers: Implement the SlaPolicy model and migration with response_time_limit (minutes) and resolution_time_limit (minutes) mapped per priority (low, medium, high, urgent). Add calculated fields on the Ticket model or responses indicating "time remaining" or "SLA breached" for response and resolution targets.
2. Activity Log / Audit Trail: Implement the ActivityLog model and migration to track ticket lifecycle events. Log ticket creation, assignment changes, status changes, and new comment additions, storing the actor_id, event type, and timestamps.
3. Queues & Assignment: Implement queues and ticket assignment endpoints under the api/v1 route group. This should include:
   - Reassigning a ticket to another agent.
   - Claiming an unassigned ticket (self-assignment).
   - Dynamic scopes/filters for "My tickets" and "Unassigned tickets".
4. Dashboard Metrics API: Create an endpoint `/api/v1/dashboard/metrics` that returns:
   - Ticket counts grouped by status and priority.
   - Average first-response time.
   - SLA breach rate (percentage of breached tickets).
   - Daily ticket creation volume.
5. Integration Tests: Write Pest/PHPUnit feature tests verifying SLA calculations (breached/remaining), activity logging on status change, ticket claims, and the dashboard metrics output.

text:
<@U0BDGF43EBX> Plan and start Sprint 4: React Frontend (Vite + React 19 + Tailwind CSS).

Break this sprint down into issues for:
1. React & Tailwind Scaffold: Initialize a clean React + Vite + Tailwind structure in the frontend/ folder. Add package.json, vite.config.js, tailwind.config.js, index.html, and setup react-router-dom for page routing.
2. API Client with Sanctum: Implement src/api/client.js using Axios or Fetch. Set up base URLs pointing to the Laravel backend, automatically handling CSRF protection, saving authentication tokens, and headers for the organization context.
3. Auth UI: Build Login and Registration screens. Registration must allow creating a new Organization (tenant) alongside the Admin user.
4. Tickets Dashboard (Board/List): Build a clean dashboard displaying all tickets in a list or board view. Include:
   - Filters for status, priority, and assignee.
   - Text search on subject/description.
   - SLA indicators: A visual countdown/timer or a "Breached" badge on each ticket.
5. Ticket Details & Threaded Conversation UI: Build a detail panel showing the ticket metadata, the Activity Log audit trail (status/assignee history), and the threaded comments. Include a form that allows agents/admins to post a reply (public) or an internal note.
6. Ticket Actions & Stats Panel: Add actions to "Claim Ticket" or reassign it, and a metrics summary widget showing SLA breach rates, average first-response times, and open ticket counts.
